docs.title: Dell
docs.text:
pnputil /add-driver "C:\Users\Administrator\<model>\*.inf" /subdirs /install

⚠️ Folder name changes per model. Replace <model> with folder name.

docs.title: Step 1 – Open Driver Resource Page
docs.text:
Go to the appropriate driver download page:

  • Dell Driver Packs
  • HP Driver Packs
  • Lenovo Driver Packs

docs.title: Step 2 – Find Your Device & Download Drivers!
docs.text:
• Search for your exact model
  • Download the driver pack (.exe)
    • ⚠️ Dell Driver Packs require you to select the "Line Of Business" before searching for the specific model eg: XPS or Latitude
    • ⚠️ Lenovo puts their most recent driver packages at the bottom of the page

docs.title: Step 3 – Extract the Driver Package
docs.text:
Run the .exe file to extract drivers.

  • This does not install drivers yet

docs.title: Dell
docs.text:
pnputil /add-driver "C:\Users\Administrator\<model>\*.inf" /subdirs /install

⚠️ Folder name changes per model. Replace <model> with folder name.

docs.title: Step 8 – Run Windows Update
docs.text: stop blanco from continuing and run regular windows update once

docs.title: 🚀 Start Here
docs.text: 👉 Go straight to Quick Start if you already know what you're doing. 👉 Go to Driver Installation for the full step-by-step guide.

docs.title: 📌 Notes
docs.text:
• Always run commands as Administrator
  • Make sure you download the correct driver pack
  • Dell folder paths change depending on model
